exercises.title: Team Health Check
exercises.description: Rate your team on Google's five dimensions of team effectiveness (1-5 each). Identify the lowest score and create one action to improve it.
hints: Be honest — this is for improvement, not a report card
Ask team members for their scores independently before comparing
Focus on the single lowest dimension, not all five

solution.code:
Team Health Check Scorecard:

1. Psychological Safety: ___/5
   'I can take risks without fear of punishment'

2. Dependability: ___/5
   'I can count on my teammates to deliver on time'

3. Structure & Clarity: ___/5
   'I know what's expected of me'

4. Meaning: ___/5
   'My work matters to me personally'

5. Impact: ___/5
   'Our team creates meaningful outcomes'

Lowest dimension action plan:
Dimension: ___________
Current score: ___/5
Target score: ___/5
One action this month: ___________
How I'll measure progress: ___________
Review date: ___________

exercises.title: Team Norms Workshop
exercises.description: Facilitate a 30-minute team session to establish working agreements on communication, decision-making, and conflict resolution.
hints: Let the team propose norms — don't dictate them
Focus on 3-5 key areas, not exhaustive rules
Write them down and revisit quarterly

solution.code:
Team Norms Workshop Agenda (30 min):

1. Communication (10 min):
   - How do we handle urgent vs. non-urgent communication?
   - Response time expectations?
   - Channel preferences (Slack, email, meetings)?

2. Decision-Making (10 min):
   - Who decides what? (RAPID framework)
   - How do we handle disagreements?
   - When do we need consensus vs. when does one person decide?

3. Conflict Resolution (10 min):
   - How do we address issues directly?
   - What happens when two people disagree?
   - When do we escalate?

Output: A written document with 5-7 norms, signed by all team members.
Revisit: Quarterly, 30-minute review session.

exercises.title: Team Role Mapping
exercises.description: Map each team member's strengths using Belbin's team roles framework. Identify strengths gaps and plan to address them.
hints: Have people self-assess AND have others rate them
Look for clusters — too many of one role creates blind spots
Plan to develop missing roles through training or hiring

solution.code:
Team Role Mapping Template:

For each team member, identify their top 2-3 roles:

Person | Primary Role | Secondary Role | Development Area
-------|-------------|----------------|------------------
Alice  | Plant (ideas)| Monitor-Evaluator| Shaper (drive)
Bob    | Implementer | Completer-Finisher| Resource Investigator
Carol  | Coordinator | Teamworker | Plant (creativity)

Gap Analysis:
- Who drives action when things stall? (Shaper)
- Who generates creative ideas? (Plant)
- Who ensures quality? (Completer-Finisher)
- Who builds relationships? (Teamworker)

Action: For each gap, decide: develop an existing person, hire for it, or accept the risk?
